name: google-earth
description: Google Earth restoration — probe, enable, and deploy. Evidence suggests kh.google.com still serves dbRoot.v5 (HTTP 200) and /geauth auth has been removed (HTTP 404). Audi NAR (variant 6) has no RANGE_ block. The simplest path may be: disableAuthKey + EOL flag flip + internet. GEMMI binaries may self-provision from Google or may need deployment from EU nav update.
author: dspl1236
scripts: ge_probe.sh - SAFE: Probe GEMMI state, binaries, network, EOL flags, variant (read-only)
ge_enable.sh - PRIMARY: Add disableAuthKey to drivers.ini — may be all that is needed
ge_deploy.sh - FALLBACK: Deploy GEMMI binaries from SD card if self-provisioning fails
ge_restore.sh - SAFE: Revert all changes made by enable/deploy
recommended_flow: 1. Run ge_probe.sh — share report with community
4. Check if Google Earth appears in NAV map settings
6. If NO: run ge_deploy.sh with GEMMI binaries on SD card
research_findings: kh.google.com/dbRoot.v5 returns HTTP 200 (16KB, server ALIVE April 2026)
geFreeLoginServer — free login mode exists in binary
GEMMI binaries live at /mnt/nav/gemmi/ (NAV HDD, writable)
EU nav updates include GEMMI; NAR do not
Audi NAR (HU_VARIANT=6): NO RANGE_ block on Google Earth
VW (HU_VARIANT=15): RANGE_ blocked, gemmi_final runs independently
Congo: US RNS-850 works as long as internet works
Self-provisioning: GEE protocol may download binaries automatically
risks: EXPERIMENTAL: binary analysis + live server testing, NOT car-tested
ge_enable.sh modifies drivers.ini on writable EFS (backed up)
ge_deploy.sh copies to /mnt/nav/gemmi/ on writable HDD
NO IFS, lsd.jxe, or firmware modified by any script
